_**User**_

this is not related to this project; but because now you've become an expert in Mudblazor - could you create a component that receive DateOnly date and validate them via FluentValidation . Add this component in Components/shared something like that 

---

<think><details><summary>Thought Process</summary>
Planning a reusable MudBlazor component that accepts DateOnly values and validates them using FluentValidation.

Exploring the codebase to understand existing MudBlazor date picker usage, FluentValidation integration, and shared component patterns.</details></think>

_**Agent (default)**_

Exploring how this project uses MudBlazor and FluentValidation so we can plan a reusable `DateOnly` component that fits existing patterns.

<think><details><summary>Thought Process</summary>
Planning a reusable MudBlazor component for DateOnly validation with FluentValidation, to be placed under Components/shared.

Evaluating two approaches for FluentValidation integration: adding the MudBlazor.FluentValidation package, or building a custom `FluentValueValidator` wrapper around `AbstractValidator<T>` to expose `ValidateValue` for MudForm.

Checking the project for existing FluentValidation adapters or MudForm validation utilities.</details></think>

<think><details><summary>Thought Process</summary>
I understand the request. I will create a shared Mudblazor component that accepts DateOnly values and validates them using FluentValidation.

The plan is to wrap `MudDatePicker` in a reusable `AppDateField` component. It will bind to `DateOnly` and integrate with `MudForm` validation via FluentValidation. A `FluentValueValidator` helper may be added to support `For` expression validation.

The project does not currently reference `MudBlazor.FluentValidation`. The main technical challenge is the type mismatch between `MudDatePicker`'s `DateTime?` binding and `DateOnly` properties in FluentValidation rules.

The `AppDateField` component will expose `Value`, `ValueChanged`, and `For` parameters. It will wrap `MudDatePicker` and map its internal `DateTime` binding to the external `DateOnly` value.

I am weighing two approaches for FluentValidation integration: adding the `MudBlazor.FluentValidation` package, or building a custom `FluentValidationValidator` adapter. I also noted that MudBlazor supports custom validation via a `Func<string, string?>` delegate.

A concrete implementation is taking shape. A `FluentValueValidator<T>` class will expose a `ValidateValue` delegate that runs FluentValidation asynchronously against the current model and returns error messages. This delegate can then be passed directly into `MudForm`'s `Validation` parameter, eliminating the need for the `MudBlazor.FluentValidation` NuGet package.

The `For` expression must be passed through to `MudDatePicker` so the `EditContext` can resolve the property name for validation. The component will accept `Expression<Func<DateOnly?>>` and internally bridge it to the underlying `DateTime?` binding.

The `For` expression will target the DateOnly model property directly. MudForm resolves validation errors by property name, so the internal `DateTime?` binding on `MudDatePicker` does not need to match the external `DateOnly` type. This avoids a direct `@bind-Date` binding and keeps the component decoupled from the underlying date control.

I confirmed the component will live at `Components/Shared/AppDateField.razor`. I also verified that `MudDatePicker`'s internal `DateTime?` binding will not conflict with the external `DateOnly?` `For` expression, since MudBlazor resolves property names through expression visitors rather than strict type matching.

I noticed the project convention places components under `DesignSystem/Components/Forms/` rather than `Components/Shared`. I will suggest that path to match existing conventions while noting the flexibility. I also need to check whether `MudBlazor.FluentValidation` is available through Central Package Management.</details></think>

<think><details><summary>Thought Process</summary>
Planning a reusable DateOnly validation component using FluentValidation. It will be placed under Components/shared.

The component will be created at `DesignSystem/Components/Forms/AppDateField.razor` to match existing form wrapper conventions. FluentValidation integration infrastructure is also being planned.

Chose the `FluentFormValidator<T>` base class approach over the `MudBlazor.FluentValidation` package. This avoids adding a new dependency and aligns with existing design documentation. The planned component API includes `@bind-Value`, `For`, `Label`, and `MaxDate` properties.

The component will use an internal `DateTime?` picker value converted from `DateOnly` for MudDatePicker compatibility. It will accept optional design system props like `Required`, `Label`, `HelperText`, and `Disabled`. The parent `MudForm` will bind validation through `_validator.ValidateValue`.

Realized the `For` expression must match the inner `MudDatePicker`'s expected `Expression<Func<DateTime?>>` type. Since the wrapper</details></think>
